name: Larry Steele
abstract: Larry Steele (1913 – 1980) was an American songwriter, composer, and impresario, notable for his all-black variety revue shows. Steele was dubbed the "Black Flo Ziegfeld" for his dazzling productions.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Don Albert
about_quotes.evidence: I also met Ziggy Johnson, who was the big director of the first year, was producer from Larry Steele. I always thought that Ziggy was even a better producer than Larry Steele was.

about_quotes.speaker: Don Albert
about_quotes.evidence: He was just like Larry Steele and those guys who don't know anything about him. I only heard their name.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: Larry Steele had that group at Cafe Harlem – whatever it was – every summer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
